name: Maggotini Alfredo
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook spaghettini according to package directions.
2. Carefully drain the pasta through a colander over the sink.
3. Rinse the pasta in the colander with fresh hot water, then drain thoroughly and return it to the pot.
4. Add three tablespoons butter to the pot, stir, and cover.
5. Pour the beans (maggot eggs) and their liquid into a small saucepan and cook on medium heat with an adult's help. When the liquid comes to a boil, carefully drain. Set aside.
6. Place the remaining butter in a second saucepan and melt over low heat. Add salt and pepper, then gradually stir in half and half until it thickens and becomes slimelike.
7. Add the beans to the sauce, stirring until they are well coated.
8. Arrange the warm pasta in a serving dish, pour the slimed maggot eggs over the pasta, and use two forks to gently toss together. Serve. Serves 6 larvae lovers.
